page: Amazon Web Services c8g.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. This arm64-architecture instance features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, includes a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps, and does not feature local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across several compute and memory-intensive workloads. The c8g.16xlarge achieves top-tier status in multi-core Geekbench testing, software compilation with Clang, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory subsystem performance is also highly rated, with both cached and uncached read-only memory bandwidth, as well as large-block write bandwidth, placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Database operations and Redis SET operations similarly reach top-tier throughput levels, alongside static web serving capabilities.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated arm64 architecture delivers high resource density and high-throughput memory performance without the overhead of hyperthreading. This makes the instance highly efficient for parallelized compute tasks. The server is optimized for workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded data decompression. It also demonstrates strong performance in medium-model LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server utilizing the arm64 architecture. Powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, the instance features 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark evaluations place this server in the top tier for multi-core processing, software compilation, and memory bandwidth. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving. The c8g.16xlarge is highly efficient for parallelized, compute-intensive workloads, making it an optimal fit for software development pipelines, high-throughput web serving, database hosting, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.16xlarge is an arm64 compute-optimized server with 64 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core, memory bandwidth, and database performance.

tagline: An arm64 compute-optimized instance featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and top-tier memory bandwidth for high-throughput database and compilation workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in software compilation, database operations, and web serving
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for parallelized compute, database hosting, and multi-threaded workloads
